image_dependency: Question explicitly asks for the SMILES of the structure "shown in the image".
Selecting among close SMILES options depends on reading visual chemical structure details from the image.
multi_step: Solver must interpret the depicted transition-state structure while ignoring arrows, then map it to one of four candidate SMILES strings.
Choices differ by substituent placement and presence of functional groups, so matching requires more than simple lookup.
Task is still relatively constrained because it is multiple-choice with only four options.
verifiability: Answer is a short, explicit SMILES string and also corresponds directly to one multiple-choice option.
Correctness can be verified by exact string/choice match, though underlying chemical-structure interpretation remains image-dependent.

risk_flags: Close structural isomer options may make scoring sensitive to small visual parsing errors

decision_reasons: Question text, answer, and a primary image asset are all present, so the core semantics of this strongly image-dependent item remain recoverable.
The sample has high verifiability because the answer is a short explicit SMILES string that can be checked directly.
There are metadata inconsistencies around image fields and some risk from visually similar options, but these are quality risks rather than evidence of missing core assets.
